Command.Name: promote
Command.Description: Promote packages and their dependencies from one feed to another.

Command.Name: package
Command.Description: Promotes the specified package and its dependencies from one feed to another.

Argument.Name: id
Argument.Description: Id of the package to promote.

Option.Description: Always resolve dependencies of a package, even if the package itself exists in the destination repository. This option allows to restore the integrity of the destination repository by promoting missing dependencies.
Password to access the destination repository under the specified username.
Username with access to the destination repository.
Evaluate packages to promote, but don't actually promote them.

Push packages even if they are already exist in the destination repository. Use this option to restore the integrity of packages in the destination repository (i.e., when some packages are broken). When using this option, `--always-resolve-deps` must also be specified.
Version of the package. If not specified or set to 'latest', the most recent version will be promoted.

Command.Name: list
Command.Description: Promotes packages listed in the specified file, and their dependencies.

Argument.Name: file
Argument.Description: Path of a file with a list of packages. Each line contains package id and its version or version range. Allowed formats: - Space-separated: <id> <version/version-range> - Package Manager: Install-Package <id> -Version <version/version-range> - PackageReference: <PackageReference Include="<version>" Version="<version/version-range>" />

Option.Description: Always resolve dependencies of a package, even if the package itself exists in the destination repository. This option allows to restore the integrity of the destination repository by promoting missing dependencies.
Password to access the destination repository under the specified username.
Username with access to the destination repository.
Evaluate packages to promote, but don't actually promote them.

Push packages even if they are already exist in the destination repository. Use this option to restore the integrity of packages in the destination repository (i.e., when some packages are broken). When using this option, `--always-resolve-deps` must also be specified.

Command.Name: from-config
Command.Description: Promotes packages as configured in the specified file.

Argument.Name: file
Argument.Description: Path to the configuraton file (in YAML format). Example: packages: - id: System.Runtime versions: 4.3.1 - id: System.Text.Json versions: - '[[6, 7)' - '[[8, 9)'

Option.Description: Always resolve dependencies of a package, even if the package itself exists in the destination repository. This option allows to restore the integrity of the destination repository by promoting missing dependencies.
Password to access the destination repository under the specified username.
Username with access to the destination repository.
Evaluate packages to promote, but don't actually promote them.

Push packages even if they are already exist in the destination repository. Use this option to restore the integrity of packages in the destination repository (i.e., when some packages are broken). When using this option, `--always-resolve-deps` must also be specified.
